content: If we treat the language learning vocabulary acquisition process (rooted in the linguistic definition of reflexion as repeated meaning reinforcement) as a template for LLM self-reflection, we can build a multi-perspective self-reflection mechanism that anchors inconsistent solving outputs to consistent core task definitions, just as language learners repeatedly reinforce the core meaning of 'reflexion' across different learning contexts. This framework would resolve the common failure mode of current LLM self-reflection where agents drift into incorrect self-correction by retaining a fixed core anchor for the task goal.
